Title: Ramanan congratulates Vijay, likens Tamil Nadu victory to Anwar's political journey
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has congratulated C. Joseph Vijay on his appointment as Tamil Nadu chief minister, likening his rise to the political journey of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Human Resources minister said the film star-turned-politician's victory symbolised the resurgence of people's struggle and showed perseverance and courage in upholding principles would ultimately earn a strong mandate from society.
"Vijay's political journey reminds me of the long struggle of Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who endured various challenges, pressures and political persecution for more than two decades before eventually being appointed as Malaysia's 10th prime minister.
"Both leaders have demonstrated that struggles rooted in sincerity, sacrifice and the courage to defend the voice of the people will ultimately prevail," he said in a statement today.
Ramanan, who is PKR vice-president and Sungai Buloh member of parliament, said Vijay's rise from the entertainment industry also reflected the people's confidence in a leader who was close to the community and understood the aspirations of the grassroots.
He said the victory serves as a new chapter for Tamil Nadu in strengthening its agenda for development, unity and the well-being of its people.
"I also hope that relations and cooperation between Malaysia and Tamil Nadu can be further strengthened in various fields, including the economy, education, human capital development and culture, given the long-standing and close historical and cultural ties shared by both sides.
"The deeply rooted relationship that has long existed between the Malaysian community and the people of Tamil Nadu is an important strength that must continue to be preserved for the benefit of future generations," he said.
Vijay was
sworn in
as Tamil Nadu chief minister on Sunday after securing the support of several parties to achieve a legislative majority, following Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam's (TVK) win of 108 seats in April's state election.
Although TVK fell short of the 118 seats needed for a simple majority in the 234-member assembly, support from the Indian National Congress, Communist Party of India, Communist Party of India-Marxist, Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi and Indian Union Muslim League paved the way for the formation of the new government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WHO chief warns hantavirus fight 'not over' after cruise ship evacuation
Article:
MADRID: World Health Organization chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Tuesday "our work is not over" to contain hantavirus after evacuations from a cruise ship hit by a deadly outbreak of the illness.
The fate of the MV Hondius has sparked international alarm after three passengers died in an outbreak of the rare virus, for which no vaccines or specific treatments exist.
Yet health officials have stressed that the global public health risk is low and rejected comparisons to the start of the Covid-19 pandemic.
"There is no sign that we are seeing the start of a larger outbreak," Tedros told a joint news conference with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez in Madrid after overseeing the evacuation in Spain's Canary Islands.
"But of course the situation could change, and given the long incubation period of the virus, it's possible we might see more cases in the coming weeks," Tedros said of the Andes variant, which is transmissible between humans.
Among living patients, all of whom are passengers or crew of the ship, seven cases have been confirmed and an eighth is listed as "probable", according to an AFP tally of official figures.
The affected nationalities include the United States, Britain, France, Spain, Switzerland and the Netherlands.
More than 120 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius were flown out from Spain's Canary Islands on Sunday and Monday, and countries have adopted different health measures for their returning evacuees.
Most have followed the WHO's guidelines, which include a 42-day quarantine and constant monitoring of high-risk contacts because the incubation period can take six weeks.
- 'FOLLOW THE ADVICE' -
Tedros said he hoped countries would "follow the advice and recommendations we are making," acknowledging that nations were free to decide their own health protocols.
French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu called on Tuesday for "closer coordination" on health protocols within the European Union.
The MV Hondius presented diplomatic challenges as different countries negotiated over who would receive it and treat its passengers.
Cape Verde refused to receive the ship, which remained anchored offshore the capital Praia as three people were evacuated to Europe by air last week.
Spain allowed the vessel to anchor off the Canary Islands for the evacuation of passengers and crew on Sunday and Monday, but the Atlantic archipelago's regional government fiercely opposed the measure.
Defending his government's policy, Sanchez said the "world does not need more selfishness or more fear. What it needs are countries that show solidarity and want to step forward."
The MV Hondius left the island of Tenerife with a skeleton crew on Monday and will be disinfected upon arrival in the Netherlands on Sunday.
Hantavirus spreads from the urine, faeces and saliva of infected rodents and is endemic in Argentina, where the MV Hondius set sail on April 1 for a cruise across the Atlantic Ocean to Cape Verde.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel sent Iron Dome air defence systems to UAE, US envoy says
Article:
TEL AVIV: Israel has sent batteries for its Iron Dome air defence system along with personnel to operate them to the United Arab Emirates, US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee said on Tuesday.
The batteries, which typically include radars and missile launchers, were sent to the UAE to help it defend against Iranian attacks, a source briefed on the matter told Reuters.
Iran has targeted the UAE more than any other country during the war that began on Feb 28 with US and Israeli strikes on Iran, with Iran continuing to launch attacks at the UAE during the current truce.
"Israel just sent them (the UAE) Iron Dome batteries and personnel to help them operate them. How come?
"Because there's an extraordinary relationship between the UAE and Israel based on the Abraham Accords," Huckabee said at an event in Tel Aviv, referring to a 2020 deal that saw Israel establish ties with the UAE.
Israel has also supplied air defence systems to the UAE in the past.
Senior Emirati official Anwar Gargash, an adviser to the country's president, said on March 17 that Iranian attacks on its Arab neighbours would strengthen relations between Israel and those Arab states that have diplomatic ties with Israel.
President Donald Trump said on Monday the ceasefire with Iran was "on life support."
Huckabee, speaking in Tel Aviv, was also asked about violent attacks carried out by Israelis against Christians in Jerusalem's Old City and by Jewish settlers against Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank.
An Evangelical Christian who has long supported Israel, Huckabee responded that "terrorism is terrorism. It doesn't matter who does it, it's what is done that is the crime."
Huckabee also said many of the Israelis committing violence in the West Bank, which he referred to as Judea and Samaria, the biblical name used by Israel's government, did not live there.
He said that they may live in Tel Aviv, a city that is generally perceived as more liberal than other parts of the country.
But after some in the crowd jeered, Huckabee added: "But they probably don't."
"But wherever they live, when they commit an act of terror or crime, then what they have done, i feel like, is a dishonour to the State of Israel and to the Jewish people, and it should be condemned," he said, which was met with applause.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam Baki reflects on family sacrifice as he ends 42-year MACC career
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Tan Sri Azam Baki ended his 42-year enforcement career today, reflecting on decades of public service and paying tribute to his family.
Fighting back tears during his farewell and clocking-out ceremony at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters here, Azam said his family had faced challenges they "should never have had to face".
"My family has always stood behind me.
"They went through many hardships and trials because of my responsibilities and duties.
"I always reminded them not to be overly worried because life is not meant to be feared, but managed wisely," he said.
Azam said he remained grateful that his family stayed patient and composed through the difficult moments of his career.
The outgoing MACC chief commissioner, who turned 63 today, said the agency must continue under new leadership and stressed that loyalty to the institution was more important than loyalty to individuals.
He reminded officers that refusing to cooperate with incoming leaders would ultimately damage the organisation.
"In any organisation, discipline and loyalty towards leadership are important because we remain here to serve the rakyat and the country," he said.
Azam also said MACC's achievements were built on teamwork and collective commitment, particularly in addressing increasingly complex corruption and financial crime trends.
He described his decades in enforcement, beginning from the Anti-Corruption Agency (BPR), as a long journey of lessons and experience, but said preserving the institution's relevance remained his priority.
"The organisation must remain relevant as envisioned by earlier leadership. I only continued the legacy left behind for the nation and rakyat," he said.
Azam also apologised to MACC officers and staff for any weaknesses during his tenure, saying any shortcomings were his responsibility.
Despite retiring, Azam said he intended to continue contributing to society, particularly in governance and integrity, if his experience was needed.
He also dismissed speculation about entering politics or business after retirement, saying he had not accepted any outside offers.
In his final message to the media, Azam thanked journalists from mainstream and digital platforms for their support and engagement with MACC over the years.
He said transparency and openness had always guided his leadership, adding that leaders must be willing to face public scrutiny.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Don't sabotage the new chief,' Azam Baki tells MACC staff in farewell address
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: On his final day as chief commissioner, Tan Sri Azam Baki today had a message for Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) officers and staff: "Don't sabotage the new chief commissioner."
In his farewell address after six years at the helm, Azam called on the commission to give their full support to the new leadership and senior management to ensure continuity in the agency's administration and legacy.
"Do not sabotage the new chief commissioner because if you do, it means you are sabotaging your own organisation," he said during a special appreciation ceremony.
"Please give your support to the MACC top management because they will continue our legacy."
"In any organisation, we must remain loyal to the leadership."
Azam officially capped a 42-year career in public service, which began with the former Anti-Corruption Agency (BPR) before its transformation into the MACC.
He added that loyalty and cooperation toward the incoming leadership were crucial in ensuring continuity within the organisation and preserving the legacy built by previous leaders.
"This organisation must remain relevant as envisioned by the earlier leadership. I am only continuing the legacy left behind for the sake of the country and the people," he said.
Azam also reminded MACC officers that their responsibility to serve the rakyat and the nation remains constant, regardless of who leads the commission.
"We remain here as officers who serve the rakyat and the nation," he added.
In an emotional farewell address, Azam extended his gratitude to the organising committee and MACC officers for the send-off organised in conjunction with his retirement at the age of 63.
"I am very touched and deeply moved by the support shown by my officers in accompanying me on my retirement," he said.
Reflecting on his four decades in enforcement and public service, Azam described his career journey as one filled with invaluable experiences and challenges. He also indicated his intention to continue contributing to society after leaving office.
"I will continue working and serving the people for as long as I have the energy. I will also try to contribute based on my experience in promoting good governance and integrity wherever needed," he said.
During Azam's tenure, the MACC was involved in several major corruption and asset recovery investigations, including ongoing efforts linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal and various cross-border financial crime probes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vijay thanks Anwar for congratulatory message
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Film star-turned-politician C. Joseph Vijay has expressed appreciation to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim after receiving congratulatory remarks following his electoral success in Tamil Nadu.
Vijay said he was deeply honoured by the warm congratulations extended by Anwar and thanked him for the gracious gesture.
He added that he remained committed to fostering greater cooperation and strengthening the bond of friendship between Tamil Nadu and Malaysia.
The response came after
Anwar earlier congratulated Vijay
on his election as chief minister of Tamil Nadu, in a post on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ECRL passenger service to run shuttle trains along east coast states
Article:
KUANTAN: Passengers on the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) will be served by a state-based shuttle system rather than direct services between Gombak and Kota Baru, an official said.
Pahang Public Works, Transport and Health Committee chairman Datuk Ir Razali Kassim said the electric trains will not operate directly from Gombak to Kota Baru, Kelantan.
He said shuttle trains will stop in all three east coast states, namely Pahang, Terengganu and Kelantan.
"The electric trains will depart from Gombak to Kuantan before returning to Gombak. The same applies to services covering Terengganu and Kelantan.
"There will be multiple shuttle train trips daily, allowing passengers to secure seats more easily. This ensures fair seat allocation for all passengers," he said during the question-and-answer session at the Pahang state assembly sitting at Wisma Sri Pahang today.
Earlier, Hassan Omar (PAS–Lanchang) raised concerns over seat availability, saying each train can carry about 400 passengers despite serving routes between Gombak and Pahang, Terengganu and Kelantan.
He sought clarification on whether there would be sufficient seats for passengers boarding in Pahang, given that the service will operate from Gombak to Kota Baru and vice versa.
It is learnt that shuttle trains will operate along designated routes covering all three east coast states, while a limited number of electric trains will cover the entire 665km stretch between Gombak and Kota Baru daily, with capacity to be increased during the festive season.
Meanwhile, Razali said Kuantan and Bentong have been identified as key locations for integrated transit hub projects and public transport facilities aligned with the ECRL.
He said an integrated terminal with a commercial centre will be built near the Bentong ECRL station, while an integrated public transport terminal will be developed at the Paya Besar station in Kuantan.
The electric trains can operate at speeds of up to 160km per hour. Each six-car train can carry 425 passengers in an all-economy configuration, or 409 passengers when fitted with a business-class cabin.
The ECRL, the country's mega rail project traversing Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang and Selangor and linking Kota Baru to the Gombak Integrated Transport Terminal, is scheduled to begin operations in January 2027.
Its extension from Gombak to Port Klang is expected to be operational by January 2028.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Investors want Trump and Xi to stay out of AI's way
Article:
INVESTORS expect US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart to keep trade tensions on the backburner when they meet in Beijing and say they are focused on the booming AI sector and whether the US will relax chip export restrictions.
This is in stark contrast with years of Chinese asset prices swinging wildly on trade and tariff headlines and is reflected most clearly in the yuan, which has been steadily rising for a year to reach a three-year peak.
While thorny subjects like the US-Israeli war on Iran, Taiwan, rare earths and nuclear weapons may be discussed and major disagreements could dent market confidence, investors are presently betting on China's technology drive.
China's benchmark Shanghai Composite is trading at an 11-year high and export growth is powering ahead on a wave of AI-driven orders.
Even a widening trade surplus does not make fund managers nervous about a fresh round of US tariffs and they have swung their portfolios behind China's artificial intelligence self-sufficiency drive.
"The tables have turned. There's little China is eager to discuss with Trump," said Yang Tingwu, vice general manager of Tongheng Investment, adding that Trump's unresolved war with Iran has weakened his hand.
Yang has invested in China Mobile and China Telecom for exposure to their data-centre businesses.
The shift in market focus, with Trump due on Wednesday for his first visit to China in nearly nine years, reflects the diminished brinkmanship in the US-China relationship since Trump and President Xi Jinping pressed pause on their trade war six months ago.
US courts have struck down much of Trump's initial tariff barriers. Trade data suggests Chinese goods are reaching the US anyway via Southeast Asia.
And as the fallout from the Iran war strengthens China's push to shore up its supply chains, investors have also priced in US-China tension and bet on it spurring China's technology development.
"China has made great strides in technology, grown the new economy, expanded its global clout and increased its leverage in the global power rivalry," said Wen Xunneng, founder and CEO of Zhu Liu Asset Management.
He has been investing in AI infrastructure and expects US-China relations to be stable at least until Xi makes an expected reciprocal visit to the US.
[[nid:1438002]]
"After Xi's visit to the US, the two countries may enter the next stage of rivalry, but now it's a relatively peaceful time," he said.
During Trump's first term and at the outset of his second, the yuan traded as a barometer of the bilateral relationship.
Since the tariff storm was unleashed in April 2025, however, the yuan has been guided higher by booming exports and a belief in the market that authorities are comfortable with a stronger currency that is in contrast with a volatile dollar.
"The summit could be a tactical catalyst for CNY strength and an important marker in stabilising trade relations," Goldman Sachs analysts said in a note.
"(But) we think the case for a stronger CNY is more fundamental and longer-lasting beyond this week's events," they said, as China's external surplus drives the currency to a 12-month forecast of 6.5 to the dollar.
The yuan hit a three-year high of 6.79 to the dollar on Monday and traded near that level on Tuesday.
The expected attendance of top US business leaders at the summit has also raised hopes for deals spanning finance, agriculture, energy and aeroplanes, while some investors see an opportunity for China to push for peace in the Middle East.
"I'm hoping that maybe Trump can get Xi to put pressure on Iran to get the Strait of Hormuz open again and the oil flowing," said Jack Ablin, chief investment strategist at Cresset Wealth Advisors.
But with expectations for a big announcement low, the fallback for most traders is to watch what has been moving markets and that has been the global AI boom.
Zeng Wanping, fund manager of Beijing Monolith Fund Management, said he is interested mainly in whether the US will allow more advanced Nvidia chips to be sold in China, which will pressure local producers.
"The only thing worth monitoring is development around AI," he said. "This is the market's top focus, no other."
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Starmer defies calls to quit, says he is getting on with governing
Article:
LONDON: Prime Minister Keir Starmer defied calls to resign on Tuesday, telling ministers he would "get on with governing" despite a "destabilising" 48 hours of growing calls to set out a timetable for his departure after an election drubbing.
At a meeting of his cabinet team of ministers, Starmer, in the top job for less than two years, repeated that while he took responsibility for one of his Labour Party's worst election defeats, there had been no official move to trigger a leadership contest.
"The past 48 hours have been destabilising for government and that has a real economic cost for our country and for families," Starmer told ministers, according to his Downing Street office.
"The country expects us to get on with governing. That is what I am doing and what we must do as a Cabinet."
British government bonds rallied weakly on Starmer's comments, but remained firmly in the red for the day.
His defiance was in marked contrast to the feelings of many in his Labour Party.
On Tuesday, a junior minister resigned after a handful of ministerial aides also left the government.
More than 80 Labour lawmakers have publicly called for him to set a resignation date so the party could install a new leader in an orderly manner.
Starmer had sought to shore up his position on Monday when he promised to act more boldly and with more urgency to tackle Britain's many problems.
He had said the country would never forgive the centre-left Labour Party if it embarked on a leadership challenge, just two years after its huge parliamentary majority was supposed to bring an end to the political chaos that had gripped the country since Britain voted to leave the European Union 10 years ago.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran warns US to accept peace terms or face 'failure'
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's chief negotiator on Tuesday issued an ultimatum to the United States to accept the conditions in Teheran's 14-point proposal for peace in the Middle East war or face "failure."
The defiant message came after US President Donald Trump rejected the latest counteroffer from Iran and said that a fragile ceasefire in place since April 8 was on "life support."
But Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said Washington had to accept Teheran's "rights" if it wanted to end more than two months of conflict, as peace talks remain deadlocked after an initial round failed to produce a breakthrough last month.
"There is no alternative but to accept the rights of the Iranian people as laid out in the 14-point proposal. Any other approach will be completely inconclusive; nothing but one failure after another," said Ghalibaf in a post on X.
"The longer they drag their feet, the more American taxpayers will pay for it."
Iran has refused to back down in its war with Washington, with military officials warning they are prepared to respond to any renewed US attack.
It has choked traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz trade route, rattling global markets and giving it vital leverage, while the US has imposed its own naval blockade on Iranian ports.
Details of the latest US proposal remain limited, though media reports say it involves a one-page memorandum of understanding aimed at ending the fighting and establishing a framework for negotiations on Iran's nuclear programme.
Iran's foreign ministry said its response called for ending the war on all fronts, including Lebanon, halting the US naval blockade on Iranian ports and securing the release of Iranian assets frozen abroad under longstanding sanctions.
It did not elaborate on what Iran would offer in return.
On Tuesday, the spokesman for Iran's parliamentary national security commission said lawmakers would consider the possibility of enriching uranium to weapons-grade levels if conflict resumed.
"One of Iran's options in the event of another attack could be 90 per cent enrichment. We will examine it in parliament," Ebrahim Rezaei wrote in a post on X.
Teheran possesses a significant stockpile of uranium enriched to 60 per cent purity, with roughly 90 per cent required for a nuclear weapon.
Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium remains a key sticking point in negotiations with the United States, which insists the material must be transferred out of the country.
Iran has so far refused to move its enriched uranium stockpile abroad and insists on its right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy, though it has said the level of enrichment remains "negotiable."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy market shows signs of stability: Akmal Nasrullah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The global energy market is showing signs of stabilisation, although the situation must continue to be closely monitored due to the risk of new developments affecting energy and production costs.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said between May 5 and May 8, the average Brent crude price fell 8.5 per cent to US$108.40 per barrel compared with US$118.50 per barrel in the previous week.
However, Akmal said the level remains significantly higher than the US$68.84 per barrel recorded before the global energy crisis erupted on Feb 26, 2026.
"This development shows that the global energy market is displaying signs of stability, but it still needs to be monitored as any new disruptions could affect energy and production costs," he said during a virtual briefing on the global supply crisis today.
From a macroeconomic perspective, Akmal said Malaysia's economic position remains stable, with the advance estimate for gross domestic product in the first quarter of 2026 indicating a growth of 5.3 per cent.
He also noted that the country's headline and core inflation averaged 1.6 per cent and 2.1 per cent, respectively.
"These indicate that the nation's economy remains on a positive growth trajectory, with price pressures remaining under control," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Shut it down': Ex-MAG chief Capt Izham says bankruptcy was considered during airline's darkest period
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) managing director Datuk Captain Izham Ismail revealed that bankruptcy was among several options considered as part of efforts to restructure Malaysia Airlines, alongside adopting recovery models used by other international carriers.
He said he had discussed it with the airline's chief financial officer, Boo Hui Yee, given that the airline was in dire financial and operational condition when Izham took over in 2017, burdened by a RM25.7 billion debt, negative cash flow and poor aircraft performance.
"When I met Tun (Dr Mahathir Mohamad) before Covid-19, he asked me what we should do with Malaysia Airlines.
"I asked him if the government had the courage to go with me. If not, I told Tun, 'Shut it down,' and he looked at me and said, 'You're the first CEO to ask me to shut down Malaysia Airlines.'
"If the government doesn't have the courage to support me — I'm not asking for money, I'm asking for support — then shut it down," he said during the Faculty CEO podcast co-hosted by former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin at UCSI University here today.
He said turning around the airline required not only aggressive reforms, but also a shift in his own mindset as a leader.
"I decided to free myself from protecting my seat. Because I felt very strongly that if I continue to protect my seat, I will not be able to lead with courage to make difficult decisions," he said.
At the time, Malaysia Airlines' on-time performance was hovering at only 67 per cent, while more than 10 aircraft were grounded because the company could not afford replacement components.
Izham said the organisation had also become deeply dysfunctional, with employees clinging to nostalgia over the airline's past glory instead of confronting present realities.
"The organisation was toxic. Everybody was blaming each other and kept on referring to the golden years of before. I honestly felt at that time, the golden years were nice, but it's gone. It's not relevant anymore."
Izham said the airline then embarked on a sweeping transformation programme centred on accountability, performance and adaptability.
Among the measures introduced were strict quarterly performance reviews, organisational restructuring, salary cuts among senior management during the Covid-19 pandemic, and efforts to reposition Malaysia Airlines as a premium carrier.
"I made a very bold decision to appraise our employees every quarter. What it means is that quarter one you miss your target, automatically on quarter two, you're on notice.
"And you will sit down with your supervisor, establish a short-term goal, we call it STG, of how to recover quarter one and deliver quarter two at the same time. If you miss the STG by 75 per cent, second week of quarter three, you're out.
"Very brutal, but it has to be done. The organisation needs to move forward not because of entitlement, but it must be performance-driven," he said.
Izham said he was rotated across 17 departments during his time as a management trainee, an experience that gave him a deep understanding of how the organisation operated and how decisions made by one department could affect the entire airline.
This experience greatly influenced his decision-making as MAG's former managing director, he said.
Diversity and meritocracy were crucial elements to Izham's tenure, where the number of women in the organisation increased as they outperformed men under the airline's strict performance-based system.
He added that the percentage of Bumiputera employees in the organisation also decreased from 80 to 60 per cent as the workforce became more diverse.
However, he acknowledged that the transformation process came at a personal cost, as some of the employees who had to leave due to underperformance were people he had known for many years from when he began his career as a pilot in 1979.
Izham added the company also launched 206 transformation initiatives in the first quarter of 2019 alone, although only six ultimately.
"You must have the bravery and courage to confront and fail. Fail fast so that you will move on  faster.
"In 2019 alone, we rolled out 206 transformative ideas, initiatives. By the third quarter of 2019, 200 failed and only six latched. And those six generated RM800 million in revenue," he said.
He said the company also introduced a work-from-home policy to attract younger talent in 2019.
Unity within the organisation increased when senior management took pay cuts during the pandemic to preserve jobs for lower-ranking staff.
Izham added that he also personally paid attention to customer complaints, including messages sent directly to his personal phone number by random customers.
"How they got my number, I don't know. But I entertained them and to me, these are all important feedback.
"The seat sucks, the food sucks, everything sucks. I have to start somewhere, I start with the heart," he said.
He added that outlining Malaysia Airlines' focus to become a premium carrier was a strategic move to provide concentrated focus on its goal and business plan.
Additionally, partnerships with foreign carriers such as Singapore Airlines and Qatar Airways also played a key role in strengthening Malaysia Airlines' position.
"If you cannot beat them, be their partner," he said.
On sustainability, Izham said Malaysia Airlines remained on track to meet its environmental, social and governance (ESG) decarbonisation targets, although he believed global net-zero ambitions by 2050 could be difficult to achieve.
Reflecting on his upbringing, Izham said his humble beginnings shaped his resilience and leadership philosophy.
"My mother is an immigrant from Guangzhou. My father earned 30 ringgit a month. I lived in an attap house, which was slanted.
"And behind the house, 400m through the bushes was a river, and the river was my toilet. That's the life I went through," he said.
Izham retired from his position on Jan 31 this year and was also appointed adjunct professor at the graduate school of business at UCSI University.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang assembly erupts over zakat fund governance, alleged abuse of power
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang assembly turned heated after Mohd Yusni Mat Piah (Pas-Penaga) alleged abuse of power, weak governance and poor transparency in agencies linked to the Penang Islamic Religious Council (MAINPP).
The opposition lawmaker questioned a RM1 million allocation to a company, stressing that zakat funds must strictly benefit the eight asnaf groups recognised under Islamic law.
"Zakat has clear categories of recipients. It cannot simply be channelled elsewhere without proper justification and transparency," he said.
Yusni also demanded clarification over land acquisitions involving zakat funds and questioned whether zakat money had been used to finance an official trip to Egypt in February 2025.
He also referred to allegations involving RM100,000 allegedly deposited into a personal account, calling for explanations to be tabled before the House.
The debate intensified when Yusni alleged that a senior officer under Zakat MAINPP was appointed despite allegedly failing to meet recruitment requirements, including a minimum age of 35 and eight years' experience.
"This is a question of governance. The conditions were clearly stated, yet the appointment did not fulfil those requirements," he said.
He also claimed zakat funds initially issued as loans by MAINPP were later converted into grants that no longer required repayment.
"The rights of the eight asnaf groups cannot simply be ignored. Why was the status changed from a loan to a grant?"
Yusni also criticised what he described as an excessive concentration of power involving the deputy chief minister I, whom he claimed holds 13 positions related to Islamic affairs and zakat administration.
Government backbenchers interrupted repeatedly, accusing the opposition of politicising religious matters and damaging the image of Islamic institutions.
This prompted Deputy State Assembly Speaker Datuk Azrul Mahathir Aziz to call for calm and order.
However, Yusni maintained that the opposition had a duty to ensure accountability involving public and zakat funds.
It was previously reported that Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid had
initiated legal action
against four people over alleged defamatory statements involving the management of MAINPP and Zakat MAINPP.
In early March, Mohamad was seen leaving the state Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission office here twice, believed to be in connection with
investigations into allegations of misuse
of zakat funds linked to a senior state government official.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prices manageable but pressure building, says Akmal Nasrullah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Food prices remain broadly under control, but rising costs may lead to pressure on consumers, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
Akmal said food prices remain within a manageable range, although signs of increases have started to emerge.
"Although statistics show that food price movements remain under control, the Prime Minister and members of the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) expressed concern that certain groups of people, particularly those in rural areas, have begun to feel the pressure of rising daily goods prices," he said during a virtual briefing on the global supply crisis, today.
According to Akmal, between May 4 and May 6, selected food prices moved within a range of a 2.0 per cent decline to a 6.7 per cent increase compared with the previous week.
He noted that beef prices fell 1.4 per cent from RM36.40 per kilogramme (kg) to RM35.88 per kg while standard chicken prices remained largely unchanged at around RM9.50 to RM9.51 per kg.
However, Akmal said several items recorded increases.
Indian mackerel prices rose 3.9 per cent from RM16.76 per kilogramme to RM17.42 per kilogramme, white prawns increased 1.2 per cent to RM32.11 per kilogramme, mustard greens rose 4.0 per cent to RM7.23 per kilogramme, while fresh coconut milk increased 6.7 per cent to RM16.88 per kilogramme, he said.
On the other hand, Akmal said signs of lifestyle adjustments have also begun to emerge.
"Average daily rail ridership in April 2026 increased to 1,08 million passengers, representing a 7.6 per cent increase compared with March 2026. Meanwhile, average daily bus ridership rose to 227,448 passengers, up 10.3 per cent from the previous month," he said.
As for electricity consumption, he said the average maximum demand for the period between May 4 and May 10 increased slightly by 1.4 per cent to 20,097MW compared with 19,815MW in the previous week.
"These developments show that some people are beginning to adjust their mobility and daily spending patterns towards more cost-effective options.
"Amid uncertain global costs, small changes such as these are important in reducing the financial burden borne by households," he said.
Akmal said NEAC had agreed for the chief secretary to the government and all ministry secretaries-general to coordinate government initiatives, efforts and assistance programmes.
This will ensure aid reaches targeted groups, especially vulnerable and low-income communities, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dutch Lady gets Pekat Group's aid in 'green' energy transition
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd (DLMI) has partnered with Pekat Group Bhd to accelerate its transition to renewable energy.
DLMI said the company had entered into a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) with Pekat Group's subsidiary Pekat Solar Sdn Bhd to deploy a rooftop solar system at its DLMI@Enstek manufacturing facility.
Under the agreement, Pekat Group will design, install, own and operate the system, while DLMI will buy the electricity generated.
This model allows DLMI to adopt renewable energy without upfront capital investment, while enhancing cost predictability and long-term operational efficiency, it said.
DLMI managing director Veronika Utami said the partnership reflects a deliberate step in strengthening how the company operates, reducing its environmental footprint while ensuring resilience in a changing energy landscape.
Pekat Group group chief executive officer Tai Yee Chee said the partnership is a testament to what is possible when industry leaders take decisive actions in building a more resilient and sustainable Malaysia.
"Together, we are demonstrating that sustainability and operational excellence are not trade-offs, but complementary pursuits that define the future of Malaysian manufacturing," said Tai.
According to DLMI, the project will involve the construction and installation of solar panels across key buildings at the site, including the distribution centre (DC), administration building and employee parking.
Once operational, the system is expected to generate approximately 4.9 million kilowatt hour (kWh) of renewable electricity annually, offsetting up to 24 per cent of the plant's total electricity consumption.
Construction is expected to commence in June 2026, with operations projected for December 2026.
The project will run under a 15-year agreement, strengthening DLMI's ability to manage energy cost volatility while supporting long-term business continuity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU to invite Taliban officials to Brussels for migrant return talks
Article:
BRUSSELS: The European Commission told AFP on Monday it plans to invite Taliban officials to Brussels in the near future for talks on returning migrants to Afghanistan.
According to sources close to the matter, a letter is to be sent "imminently" to Kabul to arrange a date for a meeting in the Belgian capital.
The visit, coordinated with Sweden, would follow two trips by European officials to Afghanistan on the same issue.
Officials are now "working on a potential follow-up meeting at technical level in Brussels with the de-facto authorities in Afghanistan", a spokesperson for the European Union executive said.
No specific date has yet been raised for the visit.
As part of a broader tightening of immigration policies, around 20 EU countries are exploring how to return migrants – particularly those with criminal convictions – to Afghanistan.
In a letter in October, several urged the EU to find diplomatic and practical ways to move the issue forward.
"In this context a technical meeting took place in Kabul in January 2026," the commission spokesperson said.
He added that the 27-member EU was now working jointly with Sweden to "pursue these discussions" in Brussels.
Such visits raise a host of practical and ethical questions, not least because they involve engaging with Taliban authorities, who are not formally recognised by the EU.
The Taliban have been largely isolated on the global stage since they imposed a strict version of Islamic law upon returning to power in 2021.
To travel to Brussels, Taliban officials would need to be granted an exceptional visa – something Belgium is in principle ready to do, in its capacity as host country to the European institutions.
"If the European Commission were to invite a delegation from the Taliban regime, that could indeed be the case," Audrey Jacquiez, spokeswoman for Belgian Foreign Minister Maxime Prevot, told AFP when asked about a possible exemption.
Beyond the practicalities, the European push on returns comes as Afghanistan confronts a severe humanitarian crisis.
Since 2023, more than five million Afghans have returned from Iran and Pakistan, often forcibly.
According to international organisations, most of them live in extreme hardship, without stable housing or employment.
EU countries received about a million asylum applications filed by Afghans between 2013 and 2024, according to the bloc's data agency.
About half as many were approved over the period. In 2025, Afghans still – by far – accounted for the largest share of asylum applicants in the EU.
But as the public mood has soured on migration, Europe has looked to scale back its welcome and started discussing how to send Afghan migrants back home.
Some countries have pushed ahead, with Germany deporting more than 100 Afghans with criminal convictions since 2024, via charter flights facilitated by Qatar.
Attitudes in the country have been hardened by several fatal attacks by Afghans in recent years, including a car-ramming in Munich last year.
Austria has followed suit, receiving a delegation of Taliban representatives in Vienna in mid-September.
A number of other EU member states, including Belgium and Sweden, are looking to emulate their example, with enthusiastic backing from migration hawks.
The returns drive has drawn sharp criticism from NGOs and the political left.
"Deporting Afghans back to a country where almost half of the population cannot feed themselves is not a migration policy; it is a decision that could cost lives," says Lisa Owen, the International Rescue Committee's country director for Afghanistan.
Green EU lawmaker Melissa Camara said inviting Taliban officials to discuss Afghan returns would mean "abandoning the values and rights on which the European Union is founded."
She urged the commission "not to cross this red line."
Other migrant rights groups fear such a visit could allow Taliban officials to identify individuals they want returned to Afghanistan, potentially putting their fundamental rights at risk.
Several diplomatic sources contacted by AFP counter that the visit is first and foremost intended to resolve practical issues – such as how to issue passports to people whose embassies in Europe are not recognised by the Taliban authorities.
During their trips to Afghanistan, European officials similarly looked into the handling capacity of Kabul airport and other technical details, according to sources close to the talks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former Malaysia Airlines CEO: Alcohol served on flights to maintain commercial competitiveness
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines continues to serve alcoholic beverages on selected international flights as part of efforts to remain commercially competitive as a premium global carrier.
Former Malaysia Aviation Group group managing director Datuk Captain Izham Ismail said the airline had to balance religious sensitivities with commercial realities, particularly in meeting the expectations of premium international travellers and global airline partners.
"I am a Muslim and I know that I shouldn't be advocating alcohol. But I (also) look at the wider economy, global business.
"It's a very difficult decision because you have partners – Japan Airlines, Singapore Airlines, British Airways, American Airlines – and they have their top-tier customers.
"So you cannot ignore that completely," he said during the Faculty CEO podcast co-hosted by former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin at UCSI University here today.
Alcoholic beverages are not openly served on Malaysia Airlines flight and only provided upon request.
Izham, who retired at age 65 on Jan 31, added that Malaysia Airlines had also introduced adjustments for Muslim cabin crew members, including allowing them the option of wearing the hijab.
He added that the airline's Amal crew, previously focused on flights to Medina and Jeddah, had since been deployed on other international routes, including London and Tokyo.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mindef to begin MRCA evaluation in 2030 to replace Sukhoi, F-18 fleets
Article:
KLUANG: The Defence Ministry will begin the evaluation process for the acquisition of Multi-Role Combat Aircraft (MRCA) in the 2030s to replace the nation's ageing frontline fighter jets.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the move was necessary as the Royal Malaysian Air Force's (RMAF) current F/A-18D Hornet and Sukhoi Su-30MKM aircraft are slated for decommissioning around 2035.
"We have the F-18 and Sukhoi aircraft that are expected to be decommissioned in 2035. This means that by 2030, we need to begin evaluating which aircraft should be acquired to replace those assets," he told reporters after officiating the armed forces Madani Economy programme at Batu 3 Camp here today.
Regarding the replacement of the Hawk lead-in fighter trainers, Khaled said the armed forces would receive 18 FA-50 light combat aircraft in stages starting at the end of this year.
He noted that the acquisition of the South Korean-made FA-50 was a strategic necessity as the Hawk aircraft currently in service utilise older technology and are nearing retirement.
"The FA-50 possesses more advanced technological capabilities than the Hawk. It will not only replace the Hawk but also complement the F-18 and Sukhoi fighter aircraft currently in our inventory," he said.
In February 2023, the government signed a Letter of Acceptance (LoA) worth RM4.08 billion with Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) for the 18 FA-50 jets.
The agreement was subsequently formalised during the Langkawi International Maritime and Aerospace Exhibition 2023 (LIMA'23).
On previous plans to acquire used F/A-18 aircraft from Kuwait, Khaled confirmed the proposal had been shelved for specific reasons.
However, he assured that the 18 incoming FA-50 aircraft would be sufficient to safeguard the country's sovereignty and airspace for the time being.
The minister, who recently accompanied His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, on a special visit to Russia, said the trip allowed Malaysia to assess the latest military technologies.
He said observing various assets, including fighter aircraft, ships, missiles, and radar systems, was vital for the ministry to compare global technologies and ensure future acquisitions align with the National Defence Doctrine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australia prepares quarantine for six healthy hantavirus cruise ship passengers
Article:
ISTANBUL: Australia on Tuesday confirmed that all six hantavirus cruise ship passengers are "in good health" and will be placed in quarantine upon their return home, Anadolu Ajansi reported.
Health Minister Mark Butler told ABC News that five Australians and one New Zealander from the hantavirus‑affected cruise ship MV Hondius were expected to arrive in the Netherlands before continuing their journey to Australia.
"They were due to land at 8.10am our time, and they'll be transited to a hotel quarantine facility while the flight arrangements from the Netherlands to Australia are finalised," he said.
The minister described the flight arrangements to bring the passengers as "a difficult task" and said they would return over the course of this week.
Butler added that the Australian government was coordinating with the Department of Foreign Affairs to organise flights with crews willing to isolate after transporting the passengers.
Upon arrival in Western Australia, the passengers will enter a purpose‑built quarantine facility for about three weeks. Authorities will then decide what additional monitoring may be required during the virus' 42‑day incubation period.
The minister said the quarantine response goes beyond measures adopted by most other countries repatriating cruise passengers, many of which require only a few days in centralised quarantine followed by home isolation.
The outbreak, involving the Andes strain of hantavirus, has resulted in five confirmed cases, including three deaths, according to World Health Organisation (WHO) officials.
Scientists confirmed the outbreak was caused by the rare Andes variant of hantavirus, the only known strain capable of human‑to‑human transmission, usually through close contact.
The WHO said two passengers who later died had travelled through Argentina, Chile and Uruguay before boarding the ship.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Drug war enforcer urges Marcos to not hand him over to ICC
Article:
MANILA: The top enforcer of former Philippine leader Rodrigo Duterte's deadly war on drugs urged President Ferdinand Marcos Jr on Tuesday to not hand him over to the International Criminal Court, but said he was ready to face justice at home.
Fearing arrest and hunkered down in his legislative office for a second day, Senator Ronald dela Rosa said he had faithfully served his country.
The ICC unsealed an arrest warrant on Monday for former police chief dela Rosa, dated November, on suspicion of crimes against humanity, the same crimes 81-year-old Duterte is accused of as he awaits trial in The Hague following his arrest last year.
Dela Rosa, better known as "Bato", or "rock", has been under the protective custody of the Senate since law enforcement agents entered the building on Monday, when he reappeared in public for the first time since November. He has denied involvement in illegal killings.
"I hope that, as the father of the nation, you will take care of all Filipinos. That is the role of government, to care for its people," dela Rosa told reporters when asked what his message was to President Marcos.
"I did everything for the country. I did not enrich myself. I worked faithfully," he said, his eyes welling with tears.
"Mr. President, (you) may one day face a situation like this. You may also encounter problems, and then you will understand, you will feel what I am feeling right now."
Dela Rosa was Duterte's top lieutenant and oversaw a fierce crackdown during which police say more than 6,000 suspected drug dealers were killed in official operations.
Thousands of users were also gunned down in mysterious slumland murders blamed on vigilantes or turf wars.
Police say those killed during operations had resisted arrest and reject activists allegations of systematic murders and cover-ups.
On Tuesday, police stood in line behind riot shields outside the Senate, where all committee meetings were cancelled following dramatic scenes a day earlier, when dela Rosa was seen scrambling for the safety of his office as agents from the National Bureau of Investigation arrived.
National police chief Jose Melencio Nartatez in a statement on Tuesday said no order had been received to make an arrest, but police remain "prepared to act in accordance with lawful directives and established procedures."
The office of the president reiterated that the Philippines is no longer a signatory to the ICC's founding treaty but can execute an ICC warrant if requested by Interpol, the same procedure as Duterte's arrest.
Asked by Reuters in a text message how soon an Interpol request could be made, Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said: "Only Interpol knows."
The war on drugs was the key platform that swept Duterte to power in 2016 as a tough-talking mayor who during televised public speeches promised to kill thousands of drug dealers while repeatedly daring the ICC to come after him.
Duterte's defence team at the ICC has said he maintains his innocence and his tough rhetoric was designed to instil fear among criminals.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Freeze on military procurement funds lifted, says Khaled [WATCH]
Article:
KLUANG: The Defence Ministry has confirmed that the freeze on procurement funds for armed forces assets has been lifted following restructuring measures to prevent leakages or misuse.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said all military asset procurement matters were now proceeding as planned.
"There is no longer any freeze because we have carried out restructuring measures that can ensure there are no leakages, misuse of funds, and related issues," he told reporters after officiating the armed forces Madani Economy Programme at Batu 3 Camp here today.
On Jan 16, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had ordered a temporary freeze on procurement decisions for the armed forces and police linked to corruption allegations until procedures were fully complied with.
Khaled previously noted that the ministry was discussing the status of the funds with the Finance Ministry to ensure critical operations and military preparedness were not compromised.
The freeze followed controversies involving alleged corruption and abuse of power linked to several former top armed forces leaders.
Meanwhile, on whether the ministry was reviewing procurement strategies following the global supply crisis, Khaled said the ministry was closely monitoring the situation.
He said the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) had requested the ministry to provide proposals on how the country's defence sector could adapt to supply chain disruptions and conflicts in the Middle East.
He added that the ministry viewed enhancing local defence industry capabilities as a priority, particularly through an asymmetrical warfare approach.
"This approach refers to a situation where two countries have vastly unequal military strengths. We should adopt this strategy by producing local products in large quantities at low cost, with the capability to integrate information technology and electronic systems," he said.
On a separate case involving a soldier who suffered a fractured skull at Kem Iskandar in Mersing, Khaled said the investigation paper had been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor.
It was reported that the victim, Abdul Hamid Talib, 25, who serves with the 22nd Commando Regiment, is currently unable to speak and relies on breathing support. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dutch hospital quarantines 12 after hantavirus cruise passengers arrive in Netherlands
Article:
AMSTERDAM: Two planes with 28 passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship, which was hit by a hantavirus outbreak, landed in the Netherlands on Tuesday and a Dutch hospital treating a hantavirus patient quarantined 12 staffers in a preventative measure.
The planes landed at Eindhoven Airport shortly after midnight, carrying eight Dutch nationals. Other passengers of different nationalities will continue on to their home countries from the Netherlands, authorities previously said.
The Dutch hospital staff members were placed into preventive quarantine for six weeks after blood and urine were handled without updated and more strict protocols, the Radboudumc hospital in the city of Nijmegen said, adding that the infection risk is very low and patient care continues uninterrupted.
Radboudumc admitted a Hondius passenger infected with hantavirus on May 7.
"We will carefully investigate the course of events to learn from this so that it can be prevented in the future," said Bertine Lahuis, the chair of the hospital's executive board.
Meanwhile, the Hondius set sail for the Netherlands late on Monday evening with 25 crew as well as a doctor and a nurse. All passengers have disembarked the ship. It is expected to arrive in the Netherlands by May 17, ship owner Oceanwide Expeditions said.
Three people - a Dutch couple and a German national - have died since the start of the outbreak on the ship, which is usually spread by wild rodents but ​can also be transmitted person-to-person in rare cases of close contact.
The World Health Organization on Monday said there were now seven confirmed cases of the Andes strain of the hantavirus and two other ‌suspected cases - one ⁠person who died before being tested, and one on Tristan da Cunha, a remote South Atlantic island where there were no tests available.
The confirmed cases include a French passenger, who tested positive after the ship docked in the Canary Islands on Sunday. French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu on Monday said the passenger was in stable condition after her health had briefly worsened.
"Our compatriot who tested positive for Hantavirus is still in intensive care in a stable condition," he said.
One of 14 Spaniards quarantining at a military hospital in Madrid has tested positive for the virus, the Spanish Health Ministry said in a statement on Monday evening, adding that the patient presented no symptoms and further tests were being done before a definitive result was announced.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-MAG chief suggests pricing overhaul amid fuel surge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) managing director Izham Ismail has proposed a "cost-plus" pricing model for airlines amid rising fuel costs and geopolitical uncertainty.
Izham said the ongoing Middle East conflict had caused jet fuel prices to surge by more than 200 per cent, placing immense pressure on carriers such as Malaysia Airlines.
"The monthly fuel bill during my time was about RM400 million a month.
"Now, I think the fuel bill a month is about RM1.2 to 1.5 billion a month," he said during the Faculty CEO podcast co-hosted by former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin at UCSI University here today.
He said the spike in fuel costs had placed airlines in a "very difficult position", as they grappled with soaring operating expenses while consumers remained resistant to higher ticket prices.
Under the proposed framework, known as a cost-plus model, prices would be based on operating costs with a fixed profit margin added on top.
Although acknowledging that average fares would inevitably increase, Izham said the approach could help create a more sustainable ecosystem for the industry.
The model would see prices charged to consumers and industries calculated based on transparent operating costs during volatile periods, with a fixed margin of between five and 10 per cent.
"I'm not an expert, I'm just putting it on the table for the great minds of our country to think about it.
"What if we charge consumers, industries, cost plus: this is your cost and during this period, plus 5 per cent, plus 10 per cent.
"Everybody wins, the economy continues to develop, and so forth," he said.
Izham said such a model could help the aviation industry navigate ongoing geopolitical instability while ensuring airlines remained financially viable and the economy continued to function.
He also warned that the escalating US-Iran conflict was affecting the global economy in a manner reminiscent of the Covid-19 pandemic, albeit in a more subtle way.
Izham said many people were unaware of the economic consequences because the effects were not as visible or immediate as during the pandemic.
"I strongly believe all of us are actually in the era of Covid today. Without all of you realising, and very subtly, the Iran-US conflict is hitting us, because there's no lockdown, there's no mask.
"The reality here, the economic issue is actually affecting you already," he said.
Izham retired from his post in January.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yakeb: Former para swimmer Koh Lee Peng has received continuous aid since 2019 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Athletes Welfare Foundation (Yakeb) clarified today that former national para swimmer Koh Lee Peng has been receiving welfare and medical assistance since 2019.
The clarification follows a viral video showing Koh in distress, which sparked public concern over her well-being.
Yakeb chairman Datuk Ahmad Shapawi Ismail said the foundation provided Koh with a new electric wheelchair on April 20 to facilitate her daily activities.
"Regarding the wheelchair issue raised publicly, a new electric wheelchair was handed over to her on April 20 this year to help ease her daily movement and activities," he told the New Straits Times.
He said Yakeb was also helping to manage Koh's treatment and coordinate appointments with a neurology specialist at Kuala Lumpur Hospital.
He said this was to ensure she continued receiving proper medical care and monitoring.
"Today, we even took her to a psychiatric and mental health clinic to check on her well-being," he said.
Shapawi added that Koh had received various forms of assistance over the years, including financial aid, medical treatment, mobility support, and even a sewing machine.
"She received more than RM400 a month from the Social Welfare Department. Yakeb provides her with a monthly allowance of RM500 for a duration of six months.
"On top of that, she will receive RM3,000 in financial aid from the Association of Malay Importers and Traders of Motor Vehicles Malaysia (Pekema) in July," he said.
He added that Yakeb had previously offered Koh a kiosk business opportunity at an LRT station. However, the offer was declined.
"We have also been informed that the deputy youth and sports minister is expected to visit Koh at her home," he said.
The
viral
video had prompted questions online about the former athlete's current living conditions, with many users expressing sympathy and calling for intervention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman hunting for bamboo shoots finds 5m python instead
Article:
BALING: A search for bamboo shoots near a rubber estate in Kampung Ketemba Luar here took an unexpected turn when a woman stumbled upon a five-metre reticulated python today.
Baling Malaysia Civil Defence Force (CDF) officer Mohd Faizol Ab Aziz said the agency received a call from the woman's husband at noon.
He said a team of three personnel was deployed to the area.
"The python, measuring about five metres and weighing an estimated 80kg, was caught safely without any injuries to members of the public or personnel," he said in a statement.
Faizol said the operation was completed at 12.51pm and the reptile was removed from the area.
He said the CDF had caught 280 snakes involving various species in Baling since January.
The cases involved residential areas, plantations, public premises and villages around the district.
The reticulated python is regarded as the world's longest snake species, with large adults known to exceed six metres.
Faizol also advised the public not to approach or attempt to catch wild animals on their own.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump brushes aside Taiwan concerns ahead of Xi meet
Article:
WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump has said he is ready to discuss US arms sales to Taiwan during his visit to Beijing this week, as he suggested his personal chemistry with counterpart Xi Jinping would prevent a Chinese invasion of the island.
The White House said Trump will bring along top US executives including his former nemesis Elon Musk and Apple's Tim Cook for a trip expected to focus heavily on the US president's hopes to ramp up trade.
China said it hoped to achieve greater stability between the world's two largest economies during the visit lasting Wednesday through Friday, the first by a US president since Trump went in 2017.
Asked if the United States should keep selling weapons to Taiwan, a key irritant for Beijing, Trump did not answer directly but said on Monday: "I'm going to have that discussion with President Xi."
"President Xi would like us not to, and I'll have that discussion. That's one of the many things I'll be talking about," he told reporters in the Oval Office.
Trump, after referencing Russia's invasion of Ukraine, said of Taiwan, "I don't think it'll happen."
"I think we'll be fine. I have a very good relationship with President Xi. He knows I don't want that to happen," he said.
But Trump also noted that the United States was "very, very far away" compared with China.
When asked for a response to Trump's remarks, Taiwan's foreign ministry vowed to "continue to strengthen cooperation" with the United States, the island's main security backer, and "build effective deterrence capabilities in order to jointly maintain peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait."
Beijing's foreign ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday that "China's opposition to the United States' sale of weapons to China's Taiwan region is consistent and clear."
- CONGRESS BACKS TAIWAN -
The United States recognizes only Beijing but under domestic law is required to provide weapons for the defence of Taiwan, a self-governing democracy which China considers its own.
Under the 1982 "Six Assurances," a key foundation of US policy on Taiwan after the switch of recognition, the United States said it would not "consult" with Beijing about arms sales to the island.
Trump has long berated allies as not spending enough on their own defence. Days ahead of his trip to China, Taiwan's parliament Friday approved a US$25 billion defence spending bill, although it fell short of the government's proposal.
Pointing to the vote by parliament, a group of US senators led by Jeanne Shaheen, the top Democrat on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, said that Trump should immediately green-light a US$14 billion arms package to Taiwan.
"We urge you and your team to make clear that America's support for Taiwan is inviolable," wrote the senators, mostly Democrats but including two centrists from Trump's Republican Party.
Trump should also state that "American support for Taiwan is not up for negotiation," they wrote.
- NEW SANCTIONS OVER IRAN -
Trump delayed the trip once due to the war he launched with Israel against Iran, which is still rebuffing his appeals for an agreement.
China is the main international customer for Iran's oil, which Trump has tried to stop all countries from buying through unilateral US sanctions.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, in an interview Sunday with CBS News' "60 Minutes," said he was unhappy that Beijing had shared missile technology with Iran.
Trump's Treasury Department on Monday issued sanctions against 12 individuals and entities it said facilitated the sale and shipment of Iranian oil to China.
The sanctions came even as Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent prepared to set up Trump's visit during talks with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng in Seoul on Wednesday.
Bessent and He have been the chief negotiators for the United States and China on all trade and economic issues.
Foreign ministry spokesman Guo added on Tuesday that China "firmly opposes" to the sanctions and would safeguard the interests of domestic enterprises.
"Regarding the Iran conflict, the most pressing tasks is to do everything to avoid the resumption of fighting rather than use the conflict to maliciously smear China," he told a regular news briefing.
Trump and Xi last met face-to-face in October on the sidelines of a regional summit in South Korea.
They agreed then to a one-year truce in a blistering trade war that saw tariffs on many goods exceed 100 per cent.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Druggie remanded for filming sister in shower
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 37-year-old trader who tested positive for methamphetamine has been remanded for three days for allegedly filming his younger sister while she was showering.
Magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman granted the remand order today to facilitate investigations under Section 509 of the Penal Code for insulting modesty and Section 377D for gross indecency.
According to the police report, the 26-year-old victim received a call from the suspect yesterday asking her to come to his room as he had something to show her.
Upon arriving, she was allegedly shown a video recording of herself bathing.
Police believe the suspect recorded the footage through a gap beneath the bathroom door without the victim's knowledge.
The woman demanded that the video be deleted, but the suspect allegedly refused.
She later lodged a police report over the incident.
Acting on the report, police arrested the suspect around 1.30pm yesterday.
Police also seized the suspect's mobile phone, which allegedly contained the recording of the victim.
Checks revealed the suspect has six prior criminal records.
Investigations also found that the siblings had been living with their elder brother following the death of their parents several years ago.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maybank ordered to pay RM166k to fraud victim over online banking negligence
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court has ordered Maybank to compensate a woman RM166,000 after it failed to stop suspicious online banking transactions that emptied money from her account.
Judge Maimoonah Aid ruled that the bank was negligent after fraudsters carried out multiple unauthorised online transfers using the 47-year-old woman's online bank account in 2021.
According to court documents, the money was first withdrawn from plaintiff Chan Yan Li's housing loan account and transferred into her savings account before being sent in stages to several third-party bank accounts between June 26 and July 2, 2021.
The plaintiff only realised the transactions had taken place on July 15, the same year, when she checked her Maybank2u account. She later lodged a police report.
Chan told the court that she never approved the transactions and never received any Transaction Authorisation Code (TAC) messages on her registered mobile phone number.
Maybank denied wrongdoing, arguing that all transactions were carried out using the plaintiff's correct username and password, while TAC codes and transaction notifications were successfully sent to her registered phone number.
The bank also argued that there was no problem with its Maybank2u system and suggested the plaintiff's banking details could have been exposed due to her own negligence.
However, Maimoonah said Chan was not an active online banking user and normally used Maybank2u only for simple matters such as credit card payments or occasional money transfers.
The judge also pointed to inconsistencies between the TAC records produced by the bank and phone records from Digi shown during the trial.
The court found that the pattern of transactions was unusual and should have raised alarm bells at the bank.
"The bank should remain vigilant. The bank has the best technology available today to detect and prevent such unauthorised and fraudulent transactions.
"Some of the transfers were also carried out at odd hours, including around 5am" she said in her grounds of judgment dated May 4 this year.
The court also noted that several mule account holders connected to the transfers had pleaded guilty in separate criminal cases.
The bank was also ordered to pay RM15,000 in costs.
Lawyers G. Revathi appeared for Chan while Muhammad Izzat Zainal represented the bank.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Wangsa Maju sinkhole likely caused by drainage system failure, not land subsidence'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The sinkhole in Jalan Wangsa Delima 4, Wangsa Maju, is believed to have been caused by damage to the drainage system rather than land subsidence.
Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is still carrying out detailed investigations together with all its utility providers to identify the exact cause of the incident.
"We found that the wall of the sump had broken and we couldn't find where the debris went." he said, referring to the damaged drainage structure found at the site.
He added that the affected area, measuring about 4m wide and 4m deep, has been closed and cordoned off while repair works and investigations are ongoing.
Fadhlun explained that there is a possibility the damage was caused by underground utility works using horizontal directional drilling (HDD), which may have led to the drainage structure breaking and creating underground voids.
"When the wall breaks, the drain water goes straight into the ground and causes a cavity," he said.
However, he stressed that investigations are still ongoing with all utility companies to confirm the actual cause and that the incident is not linked to general land subsidence.
He also said DBKL has established a special task force to handle sinkhole and pothole cases in the city, as many incidents are linked to broken sewer pipes or drainage systems.
"In many cases of potholes, they are caused by a burst sewer pipe or drainage system, causing the underground area to become hollow," he said.
Fadhlun added that DBKL will continue temporary road closures at affected locations before conducting inspections and permanent repair works once the cause is confirmed.
He also revealed that utility mapping in Jalan Masjid India is now nearly 90 per cent completed, covering about 32km, and that settlement markers will be installed to detect real-time ground movement for early action in future incidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Battersea Power Station enters next growth phase with 3.2m sq ft expansion plan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Battersea Power Station's next phase of development is set to unlock up to 3.2 million square feet of new residential, commercial, cultural and leisure space across the remaining 6.5 hectares (ha) of undeveloped brownfield land at the iconic London riverside precinct.
To spearhead the next evolution of the development, the Battersea Power Station Development Company (BPSDC) has appointed the London-based architecture and urban design practice Studio Egret West as master planner to refresh and refine the original master plan conceived more than 15 years ago.
In a statement, the company said the updated vision aims to respond to changing urban lifestyles and shifting expectations surrounding workspaces, retail experiences, leisure offerings and community living.
The future expansion marks a significant new chapter in the transformation of the historic 17 ha regeneration site, which is owned by a Malaysian consortium comprising Sime Darby Property Berhad, S P Setia Berhad and Employees Provident Fund.
With more than £5 billion invested to date by the shareholders, Battersea Power Station has undergone one of the largest and most ambitious urban regeneration projects in the UK, transforming what was once an abandoned industrial site into a thriving mixed-use district.
The continued expansion is expected to further strengthen Battersea Power Station's position as a major lifestyle, tourism and commercial hub in London, while highlighting the long-term international investment footprint of Malaysian-linked developers and institutional investors in the UK property market.
Central to the redevelopment was the restoration of the iconic Grade II*-listed Battersea Power Station building, widely regarded as one of Britain's most complex and celebrated heritage conservation projects. The landmark has now been reopened and repurposed for the first time in nearly four decades.
BPSDC said construction works for two additional buildings that will complete Electric Boulevard, the precinct's pedestrian-friendly high street, are expected to commence soon, further enhancing connectivity and commercial activity within the riverside neighbourhood.
Harriett Renny, director of commercial leasing at Battersea Power Station, said the development continues to gain traction as one of London's most dynamic mixed-use destinations, supported by strong visitor traffic and growing retailer interest.
"Having welcomed more than 40 million visitors since opening in 2022, we are a testament to the enduring appeal of in-person retail and leisure, with a focus on experience and connection.
"This approach enables us to attract an exciting mix of new brands across fashion, food and leisure, while continuous evolution draws new audiences and encourages repeat visits," Renny said.
Battersea Power Station also announced plans for more than 10 new retail, leisure and cultural openings in 2026, which will expand the destination's portfolio to over 170 shops, restaurants, bars and entertainment venues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: On final day as MACC chief, Azam Baki rules out politics and business in next chapter
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has officially ruled out a career in politics or the private sector, vowing instead to dedicate his retirement to lifelong public service.
Speaking to reporters after his retirement ceremony today, Azam addressed long-standing speculation regarding his post-retirement plans, insisting he has no intention of transitioning into a businessman or a politician, despite being frequently asked about such prospects.
“I did not receive any offers from outside. It is far from my intention to become a politician or a businessman, as some have asked me before,” he clarified.
Instead, the veteran graft-buster expressed a desire to continue contributing to the nation’s governance and integrity framework.
Azam also said that his commitment to the people remains absolute and does not end with his official tenure.
“I am a man who will continue working until the mosque calls me to be laid to rest,” Azam said, using a culturally resonant phrase to signal his lifelong dedication.
“I will continue to work and serve the people for as long as my energy is needed. I will also try to assist wherever I can, based on my experience, in upholding good governance and integrity.”
Azam added that while he remains open to opportunities in the public sphere, he is equally prepared to pursue independent work that benefits his family and society if no formal roles arise.
Azam officially ended his duties today, marking the conclusion of a high-profile chapter in Malaysian law enforcement.
He will be succeeded by Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman, who is expected to officially clock in and assume leadership of the commission tomorrow morning.

Title: Anwar orders all ministries to retarget aid and protect vulnerable Malaysians from rising costs
Article:
